article_title: B.C. Reg. 33/2026 – Victoria Regional Transit Commission

minutes_briefs.title: Victoria Regional Transit Commission sets 2026 property tax rates for transit funding
minutes_briefs.summary: The Victoria Regional Transit Commission has set new property tax rates for the 2026–2027 fiscal year, effective March 31, 2026. Under B.C. Reg. 33/2026, commercial and industrial properties (classes 2, 4, 5, 6, and 7) will be taxed at $1.9408 per $1,000 of net taxable value, while most other property classes (1, 3, 8, and 9) will be taxed at $0.3594 per $1,000. These rates fund the regional transit system in the Victoria area.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check your property tax notice for the new transit levy rate and budget for the increase if your property falls in classes 2, 4, 5, 6, or 7.

key_dates.label: Victoria Regional Transit Commission 2026 property tax rates take effect

minutes_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 33/2026
Made under: British Columbia Transit Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 16, 2026

B.C. Reg. 33/2026, deposited March 16, 2026, under theBRITISH COLUMBIA TRANSIT ACT[section 15 (2)]. Regulation of the Victoria Regional Transit Commission, dated March 10, 2026. The Victoria Regional Transit Commission orders that, effective March 31, 2026, the attached Victoria Regional Transit Commission Regulation No. 47-2026 is made. — R. WINDSOR,Chair, Victoria Regional Transit Commission. VICTORIA REGIONAL TRANSIT COMMISSIONREGULATION No. 47‑2026 1In this regulation,"land and improvements"does not include land and improvements that are taxable for school purposes only by special Act. 2(1)	For the purposes of section 15 (2) (b) of theBritish Columbia Transit Act, prescribed tax rates for the fiscal year starting April 1, 2026 are (a)	$0.3594 per $1 000 of the net taxable value of land and improvements of property in classes 1, 3, 8 and 9, and (b)	$1.9408 per $1 000 of the net taxable value of land and improvements of property in classes 2, 4, 5, 6 and 7. (2)	The references to classes of property in subsection (1) are to the classes of property under the Prescribed Classes of Property Regulation.
